# What is a governance framework?

![Board members discussing what a governance framework is and the various governance processes.](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2F33u1mixi%2Fproduction%2Fd10f4d7d595518797a75faf24acdc7deff6707e1-1080x720.jpg&w=2048&q=75)

A governance framework, also called a governance structure, is essential for modern governance and legal operations. Governance frameworks direct how people interact with the organization, regulators and stakeholders to guide and monitor operations closely.

A governance framework is a supporting structure for entity management and compliance, providing the trunk from which the various branches of compliant operations can grow. Along with [entity management technology](https://www.diligent.com/solutions/entity-subsidiary-management), governance helps companies and other organizations stay on the right side of regulators regarding filing requirements and aspects such as company culture, remuneration methods and transparency of operations.

A corporate governance framework helps to center an organization’s approach around common themes, including who has a voice, who makes decisions and who has accountability. The governance framework acts as an essential supporting structure, a framework of rules and practices by which the board ensures accountability, fairness and transparency in how the company runs and communicates with its stakeholders.

Here, we’ll explain the key concepts that underpin corporate governance frameworks, including:

## Governance framework vs. governance structure

The terms ‘governance framework’ and ‘governance structure’ are interchangeable. They both describe the system of an organization’s [governance](https://www.diligent.com/resources/guides/corporate-governance), particularly the rules, procedures and roles of responsibility that influence the organization.

These frameworks or structures most often govern people in positions of authority, such as executives and the board of directors. They dictate how they will complete the activities that drive the organization forward.

![Holistic GRC](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/33u1mixi/production/d5f88c501a07f2006d823de45db686d0b058a85f-1200x600.png?fit=max&auto=format)

### Achieve operational resilience

Discover why a holistic approach to GRC is crucial for managing risks and ensuring compliance across your organization.

### Achieve operational resilience

Discover why a holistic approach to GRC is crucial for managing risks and ensuring compliance across your organization.

## Governance structure examples

Good corporate governance structures can make organizations more competitive. With [good governance](https://www.diligent.com/resources/blog/what-constitutes-good-governance), organizations ensure all their activities are consistent and up to regulatory standards, assuring the board that their rules and systems improve the business.

Examples of strong governance structures include:

## What makes a strong corporate governance framework?

A strong corporate governance framework organizes operational, risk management, reporting and financial processes to ensure the board is continually updated. Rules and systems create a robust framework for governance, and the framework provides the structure that drives the strategic plan. A strong governance structure can:

![Good governance guide.](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/33u1mixi/production/2dd0fd4be55fb26da8305cb77d6f4001238e4e42-1200x600.png?fit=max&auto=format)

### Unlock good governance tips

Improve your governance with expert tips from our good governance guide.

### Unlock good governance tips

Improve your governance with expert tips from our good governance guide.

## 5 benefits of an effective corporate governance framework

A sound governance framework can have a far-reaching impact. Though governance is most commonly a topic in the boardroom, it has a ripple effect internally and externally. Organizations that successfully execute a governance framework can expect:

## The importance of governance frameworks and structures

Corporate governance structures are essential in today’s progressive and aggressive business environment. Corporate governance allows companies to put their positive traits firmly on display. With these intentions visible to all, companies are more likely to be held accountable for their behavior and actions — and thus more willing to distance themselves from duplicity.

Businesses today are held to incredibly high standards by investors and customers alike; honesty and openness about processes and operations count for a great deal. Both shareholders and consumers want to see companies operating with integrity and transparency.

### What are the key components of a governance structure?

Business advisory firm PwC calls corporate governance [“a performance issue”](https://www.pwc.com/la/en/risk-assurance/corporate-governance.html) as it provides a framework for how the company operates, stating that corporate governance frameworks should encompass the following:

This list provides a bird’s-eye view of corporate governance in action and conveys how it can influence business. To help organizations navigate corporate governance, [Deloitte](https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/risk/articles/framing-the-future-of-corporate-governance-deloitte-governance-framework.html) offers a governance framework that outlines the board’s objectives and responsibilities and how they relate to the corporate governance infrastructure.

## How to start building your own corporate governance framework

Governance frameworks exist to ensure that a company remains in compliance and operates within legal boundaries, meaning that any governance structure must consider the local regulations wherever the organization has entities. The governance framework then dictates the governance operating model appropriate to the organization's aims.

To start building your own governance framework, aim to answer these questions:

## Governance structure for portfolio companies

A robust governance framework guides an organization in achieving accountability, authority and sound decision-making. By contrast, a weak corporate governance framework will cause a breakdown in the stages of the investment process and affect overall economic growth. For portfolio companies, a governance structure should ensure that all shareholders can vote on governance matters. All shareholders should have a voice and foundational rights.

Portfolio companies should conduct business concerning both the shareholders’ interests and the capital entrusted to them. A portfolio company’s governance structure should take the following into account and demonstrate:

A portfolio company’s governance structure should ensure adherence to the above commitments as the organization carries out its responsibilities as a market participant.

## Governance structure for subsidiaries

It’s natural for corporations to seek to establish new entities to fuel business growth and deal with increasingly complex regulations — but with each new entity comes a heightened need for [entity management](https://www.diligent.com/resources/blog/what-is-entity-management) and robust [subsidiary governance](https://www.diligent.com/resources/blog/subsidiary-management).

Globalization raises legal and corporate governance issues at the [subsidiary level](https://www.diligent.com/en-gb/resources/blog/what-is-a-subsidiary-company) that need constant attention. To deal with this, many company secretaries and legal operations professionals turn to a subsidiary governance framework template to help get things under control.

Corporate governance framework templates provide guidelines and ideas to ensure both downstream and upstream corporate governance flows are robust and compliance-proof.

A subsidiary governance framework template can help to align processes while allowing wriggle room for local action. It provides the framework to allow those responsible for subsidiary governance and entity management the space to figure out what works best for them and their needs while ensuring the policies and practices generally conform to what the parent company expects.

This means that the parent company can focus on group-level requirements and issues, work to grow the business further and devise long-term strategies. At the same time, the subsidiaries can work out the part they need to play and how that works at a jurisdictional level.

It’s not practical to assume the parent will take care of every subsidiary’s regulatory and compliance needs; any subsidiary governance framework template must lay out the roles and responsibilities of both the parent and each subsidiary. [The Chartered Governance Institute (CGI)](https://www.cgi.org.uk/blog/what-is-subsidiary-governance-intro-to-subsidiary-frameworks) has developed an “at a glance” subsidiary governance framework template checklist to help corporations operating across multiple jurisdictions and business areas.

## Governance structure for public entities

Once an organization decides to list and go public, its compliance and governance burden increases significantly. Now, it’s no longer just a company’s stakeholders and potentially the local authorities keeping an eye on how the organization is running; once it is listed, that organization becomes public property. It’s opened itself up to intense scrutiny.

Market fluctuations will impact its share price, but scrutiny can also impact price. The movements and decisions of the board will be publicly available for access by anyone with a genuine interest, and investors will have the opportunity to ask questions and express concern about business operations. This is why public entities need robust and sound governance practices.

Strong governance structures can help mitigate some of those risks, ensuring that everyone in the business — as well as the market and investors — knows the organization’s steps. It means transparency around compliance and operations is supported by clear communications, and a paper trail can lead back to the reasons for any decisions and the ultimate consequences.

A public entity must report to the market regularly. A schedule of reporting on board meetings and accounts, management changes, security breaches, and more will be expected, as will a detailed annual report. If they don’t get filed with the exchange, then the public entity, and potentially its parent, will face sanctions and fines.

As public entities develop and monitor their governance structures, they are, in essence:

And, of course, any corporate governance framework should be subject to regular audits to ensure it’s still fit for purpose, providing the proper checks and balances.

## Strengthen your corporate governance framework with entity management software

Entity management software supports the governance framework by bringing stakeholder information and entity data to a central repository that’s easily accessed from anywhere in the world. This is critical because more board members, executives and employees work remotely, meaning that they need to be able to follow the governance structure no matter where they’re logging on from. Boards not only require ways to digitize their governance systems, but they also need software that can turn their governance structure into a competitive advantage.

Diligent’s [Entity Management software](https://www.diligent.com/solutions/entity-subsidiary-management), part of the [Diligent One Platform](https://www.diligent.com/platform/diligent-one), allows boards to keep up with today’s fast-paced climate, facilitating the swift, data-based decisions that modern business requires. Request a demo to see how software can future-proof your governance framework.

[Request a demo here.](https://www.diligent.com/request-a-demo)

# Why Corporate Governance Matters: Protecting Your Business from Risk

![Stark & Stark](https://jdsupra-static.s3.amazonaws.com/client-headers/StarkAndStark/Header.jpg)

![Stark & Stark](https://jdsupra-static.s3.amazonaws.com/client-headers/StarkAndStark/Header.jpg)

When you hear the term *corporate governance*, you might assume it’s something reserved for Fortune 500 companies or sprawling multinational corporations. After all, these entities have complex operations, thousands of employees, and a web of legal and financial responsibilities that demand oversight. But in reality, corporate governance isn’t just for the big players, it’s essential for businesses of *all* sizes.

Whether you’re running a tech startup, a family-owned retail business, or a rapidly growing mid-sized company, strong governance practices can mean the difference between long-term success and costly, sometimes catastrophic, failure.

As an attorney who works closely with businesses across industries, from small businesses just getting off the ground to well-established firms navigating expansion, I’ve seen firsthand how good governance serves as a protective framework. I’ve also witnessed how the absence of such a framework can leave a business exposed to serious risks, legal complications, and even dissolution.

## **What Is Corporate Governance, and Why Does It Matter?**

At its core, corporate governance refers to the systems, principles, and processes by which a company is directed, managed, and held accountable. It defines how decisions are made, who has the authority to make them, and how various stakeholders are considered in the process, including owners, managers, employees, investors, and customers.

While that may sound like a formal or abstract concept, governance is a very practical tool that directly impacts how a company operates on a day-to-day basis. Good governance creates a structure for decision-making, defines leadership responsibilities, enforces ethical behavior, and ensures compliance with laws and regulations. It also helps build transparency and trust within the organization and with external stakeholders.

Importantly, corporate governance isn’t a one-size-fits-all concept. What governance looks like for a startup will differ from what’s appropriate for a publicly traded corporation. But the underlying principles apply universally, specifically, accountability, transparency, responsibility, and fairness.

## **The Hidden Risks of Poor Governance**

Many small to mid-sized business owners don’t think about governance until something goes wrong. Unfortunately, by the time issues arise, the damage is often already done. Companies with weak or nonexistent governance structures face a wide array of risks, including:

Often, these problems don’t stem from malice or negligence, they happen because no clear governance structure exists to prevent them.

## **How Good Governance Safeguards Your Business**

On the other hand, a strong governance framework acts as both a guardrail and a springboard. It protects your business from preventable problems and positions you for sustainable growth.

Here’s how good governance can benefit your organization:

In short, governance isn’t about creating bureaucracy, it’s about building resilience.

## **Key Elements of an Effective Governance Structure**

While every business’s governance needs will vary, several fundamental elements apply across the board. These are the cornerstones of a well-structured governance framework:

## **Corporate Governance in Action: A Real-World Example**

Let’s consider a hypothetical example. A family-owned manufacturing business is thriving and ready to bring on outside investors. However, without an operating agreement or formal board, disagreements between siblings over spending decisions and leadership roles have created friction. Investors walk away, citing concerns about internal governance.

Now imagine that same company had invested in clear bylaws, set up an advisory board, and conducted regular strategic planning meetings with documented outcomes. Not only would investor confidence increase, but internal operations would also run more smoothly.

Good governance doesn’t just solve problems—it prevents them from happening in the first place.

## **Final Thoughts: A Smart Investment in Your Future**

Corporate governance isn’t just about policies and paperwork, it’s about creating a foundation for responsible leadership, sustainable growth, and long-term success. It’s about protecting your business, your people, and your future.

No matter the size or stage of your company, strong governance can help you navigate challenges, build credibility, and seize new opportunities with confidence.

If you’re unsure where to start, working with legal counsel or a governance advisor to assess your current framework is a smart first step. They can help tailor a governance structure that fits your business model and growth plans.

Remember, it’s neither too early nor too late to strengthen your governance. Your business deserves the structure and support it needs to thrive.

[The Importance Of Corporate Governance Documents](https://www.lawgroup.biz/the-importance-of-corporate-governance-documents)
=============================================================================================================================

Posted by [Amanda Butler Schley](https://www.lawgroup.biz/amanda-butler-schley) | May 09, 2024 | 0 Comments

Corporate governance systems are the backbone of any business structure, providing a roadmap for smooth operations and decision-making. From partnerships to corporations, these documents are crucial in defining roles, responsibilities, and protocols. They are not just formalities but essential tools for mitigating future conflicts and ensuring compliance with regulations. Corporate governance documents include bylaws, articles of incorporation, and partnership agreements, each tailored to the specific business entity. These documents outline risk oversight, long-term strategies, and director orientation and set the foundation for effective governance. Understanding and implementing these guidelines are vital for maintaining transparency, accountability, and sustainable growth in any organization. If you need assistance creating, updating, or refining your corporate governance structures, Business Law Group may be able to assist you. Speak with one of our seasoned Louisiana business attorneys by calling (504) 446-6506 and scheduling a consultation.

What Is Corporate Governance?
-----------------------------

The [Louisiana Department of Insurance](https://www.ldi.la.gov/docs/default-source/documents/compliance-seminar/corporate-governance.pdf) states that corporate governance is the system of rules, practices, and processes by which businesses are directed and controlled. Balancing the interests of numerous stakeholders, including shareholders, management, customers, suppliers, financiers, government, and the community, is essential for the functionality of this system.

A few of the critical things that corporate governance does include:

*   Establishes rules: Corporate governance establishes the rules and guidelines determining how a company is directed and controlled.

*   Protects stakeholders: Corporate governance helps protect the interests of various stakeholders by ensuring accountability and transparency in decision-making processes.

*   Ensures compliance: Corporate governance ensures that the company complies with legal and regulatory requirements, safeguards its reputation, and minimizes risks.

*   Promotes ethical practices: Corporate governance promotes ethical behavior and responsible decision-making within the organization, fostering trust and credibility.

Corporate governance is crucial for maintaining a smooth business operation, fostering trust among stakeholders, and sustaining the company's growth and longevity. Some corporate governance documents are also required to be available to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) or other government agencies and considered [company records to keep updated](https://www.lawgroup.biz/how-and-why-to-keep-your-corporate-records-up-to-date).

What Is a Corporate Governance Document?
----------------------------------------

Corporate governance documents are vital to ensuring a business's smooth operation. They encompass various legal papers that outline the structure, rules, and guidelines governing the company's activities, decision-making processes, and relationships among stakeholders. These documents play a crucial role in establishing the organization's framework.

Some of the essential components of corporate governance documents can include:

*   Formation Certificate or Articles of Organization: When forming a Limited Liability Corporation (LLC), these documents establish the business entity's legal existence and define its structure and purpose. Businesses must file a [application for Articles of Organization](https://www.sos.la.gov/BusinessServices/PublishedDocuments/365ArticlesofOrganizationLouisianaLimitedLiabilityCompany.pdf) with the [Louisiana Secretary of State](https://www.sos.la.gov/BusinessServices/FileBusinessDocuments/Pages/default.aspx) to get their articles of organization.

*   Partnership Agreement or Operating Agreement: These agreements outline the roles, responsibilities, and rights of the business owners in partnerships and limited liability companies (LLCs).

*   Articles of Incorporation: Corporations typically have these documents, which contain essential details about the company and its owners. The [application for Articles of Incorporation](https://www.sos.la.gov/BusinessServices/PublishedDocuments/399ArticlesOfIncorporationLouisianaBusiness.pdf) is also filed with the Louisiana Secretary of State.

Some may regard these documents as mere formalities, but they are crucial in preventing conflicts and promoting clarity in decision-making. By laying out expectations, rights, and responsibilities, corporate governance documents help safeguard the interests of stakeholders and ensure accountability within the organization.

Corporate Governance Documents
------------------------------

When it comes to corporate governance documents, having a clear set of guidelines and policies is essential for a business's smooth operation. These documents are crucial in outlining the structure, rules, and procedures that govern a company's activities and the relationships among its stakeholders. An experienced business attorney with Business Law Group may be able to assist you in determining which documents your business needs.

### Certificate of Incorporation

The Certificate of Incorporation is a foundational document that establishes the existence of a corporation. The certificate includes essential details such as the company's name, business purpose, location, and the number of authorized shares. The document also has the name, address, and signatures of the incorporators. This document is filed with the state and is required to [form a corporation](https://www.lawgroup.biz/business-formation).

### Bylaws

Bylaws are the rules and regulations that govern a corporation's internal management. They outline the procedures for holding meetings, electing directors, and handling corporate affairs. Bylaws provide clarity on the roles and responsibilities of the board of directors, officers, and shareholders.

### Corporate Governance Guidelines

Corporate governance guidelines set the framework for a company's governance practices. These guidelines define the roles and responsibilities of the board of directors, establish committees, and outline the principles for effective decision-making and accountability within the organization. The guidelines may also include instructions for the corporation's management, values, strategy, or operation, as well as provisions on ethics, social responsibility, or environmental or political concerns. Some businesses opt to make their corporate governance guidelines part of their bylaws instead of a separate document.

### Board of Directors Code of Conduct

The Board of Directors Code of Conduct outlines the ethical standards and behavior expected from board members. The code sets expectations for integrity, transparency, and confidentiality in board discussions and decision-making processes. Adhering to a code of conduct is essential in maintaining trust and credibility.

### Director Independence Guidelines

Director independence guidelines define the criteria for determining the independence of directors serving on the board. Independence is crucial for ensuring unbiased decision-making and oversight. These guidelines help identify and address any potential conflicts of interest that may arise.

### Lobbying and Political Activities Governance

Effective lobbying and political activities governance policies help companies navigate the complex landscape of political involvement. These policies set boundaries and provide transparency in the company's interactions with government entities. Clear guidelines help in ensuring compliance with relevant laws and regulations.

This document may include the company's political activities principle, which outlines the company's stance on engaging in political activities. These guidelines define the permissible actions and contributions that the company can make in the political arena. Adhering to this principle helps in maintaining ethical standards and alignment with the company's values.

### Employee Business Conduct Policies

Employee Business Conduct Policies establish guidelines for ethical behavior and professional conduct within the organization. These policies cover areas such as conflicts of interest, confidentiality, and compliance with laws and regulations. Clear policies help in fostering a culture of integrity and accountability among employees.

How Do You Create a Corporate Governance Structure?
---------------------------------------------------

Organizations need to follow a strategic process to establish a robust corporate governance structure. They can create a practical framework with the following steps:

*   Review Existing Documents: Start by examining current corporate governance documents such as the Certificate of Incorporation, Bylaws, and Corporate Governance Guidelines. Evaluate their effectiveness in aligning with the company's objectives.

*   Identify Key Stakeholders: Determine the relevant stakeholders who should be involved in the governance structure. This may include shareholders, board members, executives, and regulatory bodies.

*   Define Roles and Responsibilities: Clearly outline the roles and responsibilities of each party involved in the governance process. This will help maintain accountability and ensure efficient decision-making.

*   Implement Ethical Standards: Incorporate ethical standards and codes of conduct into the governance framework. These standards should reflect the organization's values and principles.

*   Establish Reporting Mechanisms: Develop reporting mechanisms that outline how information flows within the organization. This ensures transparency and enables effective communication.

*   Continuous Evaluation and Improvement: Regularly assess the governance structure to identify areas for enhancement. This may involve conducting audits, seeking feedback, and staying updated on regulatory requirements.

By following these steps, businesses can create a corporate governance structure that promotes transparency, accountability, and ethical behavior within the organization.

Frequently Asked Questions
--------------------------

The following are some frequently asked questions about corporate governance structure and documents.

### What are the key steps to create a robust corporate governance structure?

Review existing documents, identify key stakeholders, define roles and responsibilities, implement ethical standards, establish reporting mechanisms, and continuously evaluate and improve the governance framework.

### What is included in a corporate governance document?

Corporate governance documents may include a certificate of incorporation, bylaws, and often a shareholders' agreement for corporations, and a formation certificate, partnership agreement, or operating agreement for limited partnerships or LLCs.

### What are the core concepts in good corporate governance?

Fairness, accountability, responsibility, disclosure, and transparency are the core principles of good corporate governance, which promotes company performance and investor confidence.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Organizations must adapt governance frameworks to align with specific operational needs, regulatory requirements, and stakeholder expectations, ensuring effective management and compliance.
Different industries face unique regulatory and operational challenges; governance frameworks should address these to enhance compliance and operational effectiveness.
Cultural differences impact governance practices; understanding local values and norms is crucial for crafting governance that resonates with stakeholders and promotes ethical behavior.
Governance frameworks should allow flexibility, enabling organizations to adapt to changing circumstances, regulatory updates, and evolving industry standards without compromising integrity.
Regular assessments and updates of governance practices foster continuous improvement, ensuring that frameworks remain relevant and effective in managing risks and enhancing accountability.
